context: Tests whether the agent uses rate() instead of count_over_time() for alerting thresholds, applies the or vector(0) pattern to prevent no-data gaps, and correctly builds the error ratio expression.

checklist.name: rate() for absolute alert
checklist.description: The high-error-rate alert expression uses rate() not count_over_time()

checklist.name: vector(0) fallback
checklist.description: The high error rate expression uses '... or vector(0)' to ensure the metric evaluates to 0 when no errors are present

checklist.name: Error ratio uses rate()
checklist.description: The error ratio expression divides rate() of errors by rate() of all events (not count_over_time on either side)

checklist.name: Line filter before parser
checklist.description: Both expressions place a line filter (e.g. |= "error") before the json parser stage

checklist.name: Threshold set correctly
checklist.description: The high error rate alert uses a threshold of > 2 (not > 100 or another count-based value) consistent with per-second rate semantics

checklist.name: Error ratio threshold 5%
checklist.description: The error ratio expression compares against 0.05 (5%) not 5 or another non-fractional value

checklist.name: count_over_time failure explained
checklist.description: The output document contains a sentence or note explaining that count_over_time breaks when the time range changes (raw count vs rate semantics)

checklist.name: Expressions labelled
checklist.description: The output document clearly labels which expression is the absolute rate alert and which is the ratio alert
